dialogues.text: Alright, first one is from Cameron. Subject line: Loved the IMAX preview episode. "Hey Duners, longtime listener. First time emailer. I have no doubt now that Denis will elevate the source material and couldn't be more excited. You guys have got to go to the movie premiere. Keep up the great work. All the best, Cameron."

dialogues.text: That's a great, that's a great letter. We were definitely hype to have seen the movie. I've been thinking about it a lot. It was exciting. I'm glad people could hear our joy on the episode. I also want to thank Danny from 70mm joining us to share in it. It was a lot of fun.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: The greatest film that will ever be made Danny said. That was strong, strong praise for for Dune. [Jason laughs] Second email is from our good buddy Daniel McGibben. Who says "Hey Dune Pod, shocker to no one, the 10 minute preview did not make it to Winnipeg, Canada." Sorry!

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Sad for you. Sad for you Winnipeg.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: "I retained a small hope that an entrepreneurial viewer would somehow get a hand cam but it sounds like security was tight." It was. They had like the laser rangefinders and stuff in the dark.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Yeah, it was like the it was like the New Jack City scene.

dialogues.text: Ed, we were there, like they took our phones. So we had to sit for an hour and a half right? It was 90 minutes. And Jason and I had a lovely conversation.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Yeah, we talked about our families. Matt and I don't really know each other very well. So it was a good opportunity for bonding, for host bonding. [Ed laughs]

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: It was good host bonding. Where was it?

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Metreon.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: IMAX. Metreon.

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: Oh, Metreon. Okay.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Yeah, it was good. "I can't believe we still have to wait three more months. But in the meantime, you guys managed to raise the hype level to 9000 with your pre showing hot take. At least they gave the rest of us plebes, a taste of the Hans Zimmer soundtrack. PS. sounds like you converted Danny. Possible third host incoming? Daniel McGibbon." Daniel, like Danny is from 70mm. He's doing artwork. He's got an Apex streaming with slim every single night.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: We're lucky to have him for the hour that we had. It was great. We're not asking anymore.

dialogues.text: Here is our solo voicemail for tonight. And it's only, it's less than a minute and a half. Here we go. [voicemail plays]

dialogues.name: VOICEMAIL
dialogues.text: Dune Pod, hey it's Cory, just calling in for the Source Code episode. Back to the 90s, I've been listening to a great 60 Songs That Explain the 90s that's really great. And then I watched Woodstock 99: Peace, Love and Rage. Not sure if you guys have hears about this, documentary about Woodstock 99, which I remember vividly being glued to MTV, like watching the horror unfolding. Shitty ass music festival filled with shitty ass people and shitty ass bands. Highly recommend it if you guys haven't checked it out yet. I'm looking forward to hearing about Source Code. Thanks, guys. Have a great episode. Bye! [voicemail ends]

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Thanks, Cory. We love you.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Cory!

dialogues.text: Nice. Well, so that brings us to our announcement for next week. So we have been talking for a while about wanting to get our next guest onto the show. And we're really really excited to have - is this our first CEO on the show?

dialogues.text: The first time I ever interacted him was when I was at Mezzanine seeing Run The Jewels, there were about 200 people. dialogues.text: Mhmm! Get hyped.

dialogues.text: Ed. What's your take on Batman? Hot take?

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: Well, you know, actually, my main memory of Batman is the first time I saw it was we, so I grew up in Trinidad in the Caribbean. And we had a coup attempt, which was actually - [Jason laughs]

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Just a fun coup story.

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: It's a fun coup story! Well, I was nine years old and you know, everything ended okay. So whatever. But basically we have one TV station, and they had taken over the TV station, the perpetrators of the coup attempt to take over the lone TV station. And they just I guess they had a bunch of movies in the station. And so they just started showing like The Little Mermaid was one and Batman was one of those. That's when I saw Batman was like - [Jason laughs]

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: That's amazing. Wait. Wait So the coup perpetrators were like to keep people chill, we'll show Batman. Is that the, is that what was going on?

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: Yeah. Well, you know, they couldn't be on all the time.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Right. They couldn't be on all the time. [Jason laughs] That's amazing.

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: They'd like cut in with updates. And then, you know, now back to Batman. [Ed laughs]

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: It's sort of like, that's amazing. That's pretty amazing. It's sort of like, but not quite the same thing. Like after 9/11 when PBS was just like, we don't know what the fuck to do. We're running Ken Burns baseball for 24 hours a day for a week. [Haitch laughs] Hope you guys can watch that, because that's all we're doing.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Oh my god. [Ed laughs]

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: That's awesome. That's great. That's a weird time, man. That's a weird time.

dialogues.name: ED
dialogues.text: It was, it was a weird time. I mean, you know, I was, I was nine. So when they first did it, it was a Friday night, which was MacGyver night. And I was very upset because MacGyver got preempted. [Haitch & Jason laugh]

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: We interrupt this episode of MacGyver for our live coup attempt. Wow.

dialogues.text: The synergy. Just a wasted opportunity. Well, Ed, I hope you'll revisit Batman 1989 and see how it how it lands. [Jason laughs]

dialogues.name: JASON

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: I'm trying. Warner Brothers marketing. I'm always trying. [Jason laughs] It is streaming on HBO Max. So check it out. I think it's on in 4k.

dialogues.text: Oh, great.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Get ready for that. It's gonna be, it's gonna be intense. Ed, what do you have to plug?

dialogues.name: ED

They are on the American Bar Association website. You can donate to a IJP. And, you know, and just making sure that people's rights are respected in immigration court because they don't have a lot of advocates a lot of the time.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: Awesome.

dialogues.name: HAITCH I
dialogues.text: like it. I like it. Very good. Yeah. I would love the opportunity to contribute for that and help out. Amazing. We did it you guys. We did it.
